paused_reason: Codegen scope. Touches inference plumbing (OpenRouter SSE) + WebSocket routing + frontend chat rendering. Not safe for autonomous hex-agent execution per the rogue-agent incident. Resume deliberately, one phase at a time.

tasks.title: Backend: change /api/brain/chat to stream — switch handler return type from (StatusCode, Json<Value>) to axum::response::Sse<Stream>. Wrap the inference call to emit an event per token: { type: 'token', text } for each chunk, then { type: 'done', model, totalTokens }. Reuse OpenRouter's stream:true response shape. Keep the non-streaming endpoint at /api/brain/chat?stream=false for backwards compat with existing CLI tooling.

Frontend: ChatPanel switches from POST + await to EventSource (or fetch-stream). Replace the optimistic 'thinking...' bubble with a live one that appends each token as it arrives. On 'done' event, freeze the bubble.

Backend: change /api/brain/broadcast to SSE. Spawn one inference call per persona; each emits { type: 'response', role, model, content } as soon as it lands. Final { type: 'done', total } when all complete. The slowest no longer holds back the fastest.

Frontend: broadcast handler subscribes to SSE, appends a new bubble to the chat each time a 'response' event arrives. Replace the single pending 'dispatching to N personas...' bubble with a counter ('3/25 returned · 22 in flight') that ticks down as responses land.

phases.title: Broadcast progressive disclosure — render each persona as it lands

tasks.title: Add a 'cancel' button to in-flight bubbles. Frontend AbortController on the SSE request → backend drops the upstream connection. Critical for @all when the operator realizes 25 personas wasn't what they wanted.

phases.rationale: localStorage persistence (already shipped) only protects against browser refresh. It doesn't survive: clearing browser data, switching machines, or sharing a thread with another operator. Backend storage + per-session thread IDs gives durable + portable conversations. Also enables the 'show me the conversation that produced ADR-XXX' navigation referenced in the M3 design.

tasks.title: Add chat_threads + chat_messages tables to hexflo-coordination STDB module. Schema: thread (id, owner_agent_id, title, created_at, last_active_at), message (id, thread_id, from_role, to_role, content, model, ts, parent_id?). Reducers: thread_create, thread_list, message_append.
POST /api/brain/chat: extract or create thread_id, append message, append response. Add GET /api/brain/threads (operator's threads) and GET /api/brain/threads/{id}/messages.
Frontend: chat history sidebar showing past threads. Click a thread → load its messages. Replace localStorage as the primary store; keep it as offline cache only.

Cross-link with artifacts: when a chat produces an ADR or workplan draft, store the thread_id in the artifact's metadata. From the artifact view, link back to the conversation that produced it. Closes the loop on 'how did we end up with this ADR?'

phases.title: Backend conversation storage — survives browser + machine boundaries

rationale: Today /api/brain/chat returns one big JSON blob after the full response is generated — operator stares at 'thinking...' for 5-30s before seeing anything. /api/brain/broadcast is worse: 25 parallel calls all return at once after the slowest finishes. The fix is two-layer: (1) stream tokens as they're generated for single-agent chat, (2) for broadcast, render each persona's response as it lands rather than waiting for the whole batch. OpenRouter supports SSE; Anthropic supports SSE. The inference router already calls upstream — we just need to plumb the stream through hex-nexus → frontend.
title: Brain chat — token streaming + broadcast progressive disclosure
